content: The Lord sent Jonah to Nineveh, but where did he flee?
explanation: Jonah 1:3 - Jonah fled from the presence of the Lord by ship to Tarshish, the exact opposite direction.

content: When Jonah fled, what did the Lord do?
explanation: Jonah 1:4 - The Lord did not let Jonah escape successfully. He sent a great storm to bring him back.
options: Sent a great storm on the sea

content: What did Jonah tell the sailors to do so the sea would calm?
explanation: Jonah 1:12 - Jonah took responsibility and was willing to bear the consequences of his sin.

content: Jonah declared from inside the fish: "___ comes from the Lord"
explanation: Jonah 2:9 - Even in the most hopeless circumstances, Jonah recognized that only the Lord saves.

content: The second time the Lord called, what did Jonah do?
explanation: Jonah 3:3 - This time Jonah obeyed. The Lord gives second chances.
options: Obeyed and went to Nineveh to preach

content: How did the people of Nineveh respond when they heard Jonah preach?
explanation: Jonah 3:5 - The whole city repented from king to commoner! The greatest repentance in the Old Testament.

content: When Nineveh repented, what did God do?
explanation: Jonah 3:10 - God relented when the people repented. He is merciful and ready to forgive.
options: Relented and did not destroy them

content: Why was Jonah angry when Nineveh was forgiven?
explanation: Jonah 4:2 - Jonah admitted he knew from the start that God would forgive. His problem was a narrow heart and ethnic prejudice.
options: Because he already knew God was merciful and would forgive; he did not want enemies to be saved

content: With what question does the Lord end the book of Jonah?
explanation: Jonah 4:11 - God loves all people, even enemies. God's love has no ethnic boundaries.
options: Should I not have compassion on Nineveh, with its 120,000 people who cannot tell their right hand from their left?

content: How did Jonah describe God in a way that made him angry?
explanation: Jonah quotes Exodus 34:6 - God's character. God's goodness is what upset Jonah.
options: Gracious, compassionate, slow to anger, abounding in love, who relents from sending calamity

content: Jonah told the sailors: "I fear the Lord, the God of ___"
explanation: Even while fleeing from the Lord, Jonah still confessed Him as the Creator.
options: Heaven, who made the sea and the dry land

content: How long was Jonah inside the great fish?
explanation: Jonah 1:17 - Three days and three nights. Jesus used this event as a type of His resurrection (Matthew 12:40).

content: Whose son was Jonah?
explanation: Jonah 1:1 — 'Now the word of the LORD came to Jonah the son of Amittai.'

content: During the storm, what did the sailors do while Jonah slept in the hold?
explanation: Jonah 1:5-6 — 'The mariners were afraid, and each cried out to his god... So the captain came and said to him, What do you mean, you sleeper? Arise, call out to your god!'
options: Each cried to his own god

content: The sailors cast lots to learn the cause of the storm; on whom did the lot fall?
explanation: Jonah 1:7 — 'They cast lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.'

content: From where did Jonah pray to the LORD?
explanation: Jonah 2:1-2 — 'Then Jonah prayed to the LORD his God from the belly of the fish... I called out to the LORD, out of my distress, and he answered me.'
options: From the belly of the great fish

content: After Jonah's prayer, what did the LORD command the fish to do?
explanation: Jonah 2:10 — 'And the LORD spoke to the fish, and it vomited Jonah out upon the dry land.'
options: Vomit Jonah out upon dry land

content: When Jonah preached, what did the king of Nineveh do?
explanation: Jonah 3:6 — 'The word reached the king of Nineveh, and he arose from his throne, removed his robe, covered himself with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.'
options: Arose from his throne, put on sackcloth, and sat in ashes

content: What kind of plant did the LORD appoint to give shade for Jonah?
explanation: Jonah 4:6 — 'Now the LORD God appointed a plant and made it come up over Jonah, that it might be a shade over his head, to save him from his discomfort.'

content: What did God appoint to destroy the plant and afflict Jonah?
explanation: Jonah 4:7-8 — 'But when dawn came up the next day, God appointed a worm that attacked the plant, so that it withered... God appointed a scorching east wind.'
options: A worm and a scorching east wind
